notes: An atypically easygoing dwarf who has spent most of his adult life moving through mixed-culture cities and trade towns. Fights with a battleaxe and shield. Has strong feelings about regional ales.
personality: I have visited a lot of taverns in a lot of cities and I have opinions about all of them. I also genuinely want to know how you make the things you make — craft is craft, regardless of who's doing it.
ideals: Good work speaks for itself, regardless of who did it. A well-made thing earns respect, and so does a well-handled situation.
bonds: I've been away from the mountain clans long enough that going back would feel like visiting somewhere I used to know. I've made my peace with that.
flaws: I trust people a bit too quickly and have been burned for it more than once. I'll freely admit this and probably do it again anyway.

combat_tactics.role: Two-attack Champion who crits more often and hits harder. Extra Attack at level 5 doubles sustained output — every round is now two chances at a 19-20 crit with Dueling bonus damage.

tactics.text: Extra Attack (L5): two battleaxe swings per action. STR 17 (+3) + prof +3 + Dueling (+2) = +6 to hit, 1d8+5 per attack. Average ~11 per hit, ~22 per round sustained. Improved Critical crits on 19-20 — with two attacks, that's roughly 19% chance of at least one crit per round, doubling damage dice on the hit.

Two attacks means twice the crit chances. Coordinate with the party for advantage conditions: Petal's Entangle (restrained = advantage), Reverberus's Faerie Fire (advantage on all attacks vs target), Koss's Guiding Bolt (advantage on next attack). With advantage and two rolls, the 19-20 window fires in roughly 34% of turns.

Action Surge at L5 = four battleaxe swings in one turn. Use on the highest-threat enemy, especially when conditions grant advantage. Four attacks with 19-20 crits: a real chance of multiple crits in the burst window. This is a combat-ender against most L5 encounter threats.

D10 hit die + CON 14: robust HP for a frontliner. Second Wind (bonus action, 1d10+5 at L5) provides 6-15 free HP per short rest. Dwarven resilience: advantage on poison saves. No tricks needed — Jukka's value is consistent, turn-after-turn melee pressure that the rest of the party can rely on absolutely.
